acid: adjective. Sour, sharp, or biting to the taste; tart; having the taste of vinegar.
ain: noun. Alternative form of ayin (“Semitic letter”).
and: conjunction. As a coordinating conjunction; expressing two elements to be taken together or in addition to each other.
ani: noun. Any bird of the genus Crotophaga in the cuckoo family.
ash: noun. Solid remains of a fire.
awn: noun. The bristle or beard of barley, oats, grasses, etc., or any similar bristlelike appendage; arista.
cad: noun. A low-bred, presuming person; a mean, vulgar fellow, especially one that cannot be trusted with a lady.
caid: noun. A local governor or leader, especially in North Africa or Moorish Spain; an alcaide.
canid: noun. Any member of the family Canidae, including canines (dogs, wolves, coyotes and jackals) and vulpines (foxes).
cash: noun. Money in the form of notes or bills and coins, as opposed to checks, credit or electronic transactions.
caw: noun. The harsh cry of a crow.
chad: noun. Small pieces of paper punched out from the edges of continuous stationery, or from ballot papers, paper tape, punched cards, etc.
chain: noun. A series of interconnected rings or links usually made of metal.
chi: noun. The twenty-second letter of the Classical and Modern Greek alphabets.
chia: noun. A Mexican sage grown for its edible seeds, Salvia hispanica.
chin: noun. The bottom of a face, the typically jutting jawline below the mouth.
china: noun. Synonym of porcelain, a hard white translucent ceramic made from kaolin, now sometimes distinguished in reference to tableware as
cis: adjective. Having two mutations on two genes on the same chromosome of a homologous pair.
dah: noun. The spoken representation of a dash in radio and telegraph Morse code.
dais: noun. A raised platform in a room for a high table, a seat of honour, a throne, or other dignified occupancy, such as ancestral statues; a
danish: noun. Danish pastry, light sweet yeast-raised roll usually filled with fruit or cheese.
dash: noun. Any of the following symbols: ‒ (figure dash), – (en dash),, (em dash), or ― (horizontal bar).
dashi: noun. A type of soup or cooking stock, often made from kelp.
daw: noun. A western jackdaw, Coloeus monedula, a passerine bird in the crow family (Corvidae), more commonly called jackdaw.
dawn: verb. To begin to brighten with daylight.
din: noun. A loud noise; a cacophony or loud commotion.
disc: noun. A thin, flat, circular plate or similar object.
dish: noun. A vessel such as a plate for holding or serving food, often flat with a depressed region in the middle.
had: verb. simple past and past participle of have
hand: noun. The part of the forelimb below the forearm or wrist in a human, and the corresponding part in many other animals.
has: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of have
haw: interjection. An imitation of laughter, often used to express scorn or disbelief.
hic: interjection. An approximation to the sound of a hiccup, used e.g. to indicate drunkenness.
hin: noun. A former Hebrew liquid measure of volume (about 3.8 L).
hind: adjective. Located at the rear (most often said of animals' body parts).
hwan: noun. The monetary unit of South Korea from February 15, 1953 to June 9, 1962.
inch: noun. An English unit of length equal to 1/12 of a foot or 2.54 cm, conceived as roughly the width of a thumb.
sac: noun. A bag or pouch inside a plant or animal that typically contains a fluid.
said: verb. simple past and past participle of say
sain: verb. To make the sign of the cross on or over something or someone.
sand: noun. Rock that is ground more finely than gravel, but is not as fine as silt (more formally, see grain sizes chart), forming beaches and
sandhi: noun. Any of a wide variety of phonological processes that occur at morpheme or word boundaries, such as the fusion of sounds across word
sandwich: noun. A dish or foodstuff where at least one piece, but typically two or more pieces, of bread serves as the wrapper or container of some
saw: noun. A tool used for cutting hard substances, in particular wood or metal.
scad: noun. Any of several fish, of the family Carangidae, from the western Atlantic.
scan: verb. To examine sequentially, carefully, or critically; to scrutinize; to behold closely.
schwa: noun. An indeterminate central vowel sound as the "a" in " a bout ", represented as / ə / in IPA.
shad: noun. Any one of several species of food fishes that make up the genus Alosa in the family Clupeidae, to which the herrings also belong;
shaw: noun. A thicket; a small wood or grove.
shin: noun. The front part of the leg below the knee; the front edge of the shin bone: Shinbone on Wikipedia.
sic: adverb. Thus; as written; used to indicate, for example, that text is being quoted as it is from the source.
sin: noun. A violation of divine will or religious law.
sinh: noun. A traditional tube skirt worn by Lao and Thai women, particularly northern Thai and northeastern Thai women.
swain: noun. A young man or boy in service; a servant.
swan: noun. Any of various species of large, long-necked waterfowl, of genus Cygnus (bird family: Anatidae), most of which have white plumage.
wadi: noun. A valley, gully, or stream bed in northern Africa and southwest Asia that remains dry except during the rainy season.
wain: noun. A wagon; a four-wheeled cart for hauling loads, usually pulled by horses or oxen.
wand: noun. A hand-held narrow rod, usually used for pointing or instructing, or as a traditional emblem of authority.
was: verb. first-person singular simple past indicative of be.
whid: noun. A quick motion; a rapid, quiet movement, usually by small game.
wich: noun. Alternative spelling of wych (“brine spring or well”).
winch: noun. A machine consisting of a drum on an axle, a friction brake or ratchet and pawl, and a crank handle or prime mover (often an electric
wind: noun. Real or perceived movement of atmospheric air usually caused by convection or differences in air pressure.
wish: noun. A desire, hope, or longing for something or for something to happen.
ach: noun. Any of several species of plants, such as smallage, wild celery, parsley.
adh: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Adhola.
awd: symbol. ISO 639-5 language code for Arawakan languages.
chawn: noun. A gap, rift, or fissure.
chn: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Chinook Jargon.
cid: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Chimariko.
cns: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Central Asmat.
dan: noun. A title of honour or respect similar to "master" or "Sir", used of historical and legendary figures of the past.
dha: noun. Alternative form of dah (“Burmese knife”).
dich: noun. Obsolete form of ditch (“a trench”).
hain: verb. To hedge or fence in; enclose; protect by hedging.
han: verb. plural simple present of have
ida: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Idakho-Isukha-Tiriki.
inca: noun. Any of several species of hummingbirds in the genus Coeligena
isn: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Isanzu.
isnad: noun. a Muslim tradition, specifying the chain of human reporters from the time of Muhammad that authenticates the legitimacy of a hadith
nas: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nasioi.
nid: noun. Alternative form of nide (“nest of pheasants”).
nis: noun. A nix; a hobgoblin, especially one that resides in a farm house.
sai: noun. A handheld weapon with three prongs, used in some Oriental martial arts.
saic: noun. A kind of ketch heavily used in the Black Sea, Tisa, Danube and Sava
san: noun. An Ancient Greek letter representing /s/ (uppercase Ϻ, lowercase ϻ), originally used in some dialects instead of sigma.
sian: adjective. Bored, weary or fed up; tired (of something).
sich: noun. An administrative and military centre for the Zaporozhian and Danube Cossacks.
sida: noun. any of the flowering plants of the genus Sida in the mallow family
wac: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Wasco-Wishram.
waid: adjective. Oppressed with weight; crushed; weighed down.
windac: noun. A grappling iron used to bend a crossbow.
